title: DNA extraction from wheat leaf tissue
author: Benjamin Schwessinger

steps.title: Label each 2ml Eppendorf tube containing...
steps.instruction: Label each 2ml Eppendorf tube containing 2-3 steal beads with the treatment group name and your research group name. Label both the top and the side of each tube clearly.

steps.title: Carefully, study the plants in each...
steps.instruction: Carefully, study the plants in each pot for potential disease symptoms. Select two leaves for each treatment group that show representative symptoms [TG#1-4] or not in the case of the negative control [TG#5].

steps.title: Cut 1
steps.instruction: Cut 1.5- 2 cm long leaf pieces from each TG from two independent leaves. Cut the middle part of the leaves. Combine both leaf pieces into the correctly labelled tube.

steps.title: Once done with all five TGs,...
steps.instruction: Once done with all five TGs, hand your five tubes over to the demonstrator.

steps.title: Put the tube containing the leaf material on ice
steps.instruction: Put the tube containing the leaf material on ice.

steps.title: The demonstrator will freeze your sample on dry ice
steps.instruction: The demonstrator will freeze your sample on dry ice. The demonstrator will rupture your tissue using a TissueLyzer II https://www.qiagen.com/us/products/human-id-and-forensics/automation/tissuelyser-ii at 25 Hz for 1 min. Now the leaf rupture part of the protocol is complete.

steps.title: Now the leaf tissues sampling part...
steps.instruction: Now the leaf tissues sampling part of the protocol is complete.

steps.title: Label all provided tubes and spin...
steps.instruction: Label all provided tubes and spin columns with your treatment group name and your research group name. This implies to label the following set of tubes for each TG. QIAshredder spin column (pinkish color) placed in a 2 ml collection tube. One additional 2m collection tube. Two additional 1.5ml eppendorf tubes. DNeasy Mini spin column (blueish color) placed in a 2 ml collection tube. Now you will have five sets of clearly labeled tubes. This readies you to perform the DNA extraction for all five TG in parallel.

steps.title: You will receive your five tubes...
steps.instruction: You will receive your five tubes from week 2 that now contain ruptured tissue on ice. Proceed to the following sub-step as soon as possible to avoid degradation of the DNA by DNAses released during tissue lysis. The DNA will be protected in the AP1 buffer. Process all five samples at the same time.

steps.title: Add 400 μl Buffer AP1 to...
steps.instruction: Add 400 μl Buffer AP1 to each of 2ml tubes containing your ruptured tissue. Steps 5.1 to 5.7 will lyse the cells of the ruptured tissue and bring the DNA into solution.

steps.title: Vortex and completely re-suspended the tissue in the AP1 buffer
steps.instruction: Vortex and completely re-suspended the tissue in the AP1 buffer.

steps.title: Add 4 μl RNase A to...
steps.instruction: Add 4 μl RNase A to each tube and vortex again. This enzyme will digest RNA that gets released during cell lysis. It performs best at 65°C.

steps.title: Incubate for 10 min at 65°C...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 10 min at 65°C in the waterbath or heating block. Invert the tube 2–3 times during incubation.

steps.title: Collect content of the tube with...
steps.instruction: Collect content of the tube with a short "hand centrifuge" as demonstrated by the demonstrator.

steps.title: To each tube add 130 μl Buffer P3
steps.instruction: To each tube add 130 μl Buffer P3. Mix by inversion for 10 to 20 times immediately after adding the P3 buffer. Steps 5.8 to 5.16 will clear the lysate so it is ready to precipitate the DNA without precipitating too many other contaminants.

steps.title: Collect content of the tube with...
steps.instruction: Collect content of the tube with a short "hand centrifuge" as demonstrated by the demonstrator.

steps.title: Cool tubes on ice for 2 min
steps.instruction: Cool tubes on ice for 2 min.

steps.title: Incubate the five tubes on ice for 5 min
steps.instruction: Incubate the five tubes on ice for 5 min. Slightly longer incubation will not affect the outcome negatively.

steps.title: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples
steps.instruction: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples. Make sure all your tubes are clearly labelled with your research group name and treatment group name. Make sure the centrifuge is balanced before you start. Check with a demonstrator about centrifuge usage if you are unclear.

steps.title: Centrifuge the lysate for 5 min at maximum speed
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the lysate for 5 min at maximum speed. This steep will clear the DNA in solution from larger undissolved particles.

steps.title: For each of your TG, pipett...
steps.instruction: For each of your TG, pipett the 480 ul of the lysate into a QIAshredder spin column placed in a 2 ml collection tube. To not disturb the pellet when transferring the lysate. Remember! All tubes and spin columns must be labelled clearly.

steps.title: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples
steps.instruction: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples. Make sure all your tubes are clearly labelled with your research group name and treatment group name. Make sure the centrifuge is balanced before you start. Check with a demonstrator about centrifuge usage if you are unclear.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 2 min at max speed
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 2 min at max speed.

steps.title: Keep the flow through in the lower collection tube
steps.instruction: Keep the flow through in the lower collection tube.

steps.title: Discard the QIAshredder spin column
steps.instruction: Discard the QIAshredder spin column.

steps.title: For each of your TG, transfer...
steps.instruction: For each of your TG, transfer 400 ul of the flow-through into a new clearly labelled 1.5 ml tube without disturbing the pellet if present. The steps 5.17 and 5.18 will precipitate the DNA.

steps.title: For each TG, add 600 ul...
steps.instruction: For each TG, add 600 ul Buffer AW1, and mix by pipetting.

steps.title: Transfer 500 μl of the mixture...
steps.instruction: Transfer 500 μl of the mixture into a DNeasy Mini spin column placed in a 2 ml collection tube. Remember! All tubes and spin columns must be labelled clearly. The steps 5.19 to 5.22 will bind the DNA to the silica membrane that is part of the DNeasy Mini spin column.

steps.title: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples
steps.instruction: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples. Make sure all your tubes are clearly labelled with your research group name and treatment group name. Make sure the centrifuge is balanced before you start. Check with a demonstrator about centrifuge usage if you are unclear.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed.

steps.title: Discard the flow-through by pouring it...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through by pouring it into the waste collection container.

steps.title: Place the DNeasy Mini spin column...
steps.instruction: Place the DNeasy Mini spin column in the same 2ml collection tube. The steps 5.23 to 5.45 wash the bound DNA from co-precipitated and bound impurities.

steps.title: For each TG, add the remaining...
steps.instruction: For each TG, add the remaining sample of about 500 ul onto the correct DNeasy Mini spin column.

steps.title: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples
steps.instruction: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples. Make sure all your tubes are clearly labelled with your research group name and treatment group name. Make sure the centrifuge is balanced before you start. Check with a demonstrator about centrifuge usage if you are unclear.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed.

steps.title: Discard the flow-through by pouring it...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through by pouring it into the waste collection container.

steps.title: Place the spin column into a...
steps.instruction: Place the spin column into a new clearly labeled 2 ml collection tube.

steps.title: Discard the old collection tube
steps.instruction: Discard the old collection tube.

steps.title: For each TG, add 500 μl...
steps.instruction: For each TG, add 500 μl Buffer AW2 on top of the DNeasy Mini spin column. Do not touch the spin column with your pipette tip.

steps.title: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples
steps.instruction: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples. Make sure all your tubes are clearly labelled with your research group name and treatment group name. Make sure the centrifuge is balanced before you start. Check with a demonstrator about centrifuge usage if you are unclear

steps.title: Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed.

steps.title: Discard the flow-through by pouring it...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through by pouring it into the waste collection container.

steps.title: Tap the collection tube on a...
steps.instruction: Tap the collection tube on a paper towel to remove the remaining liquid.

steps.title: Place the DNeasy Mini spin column...
steps.instruction: Place the DNeasy Mini spin column in the same 2ml collection tube.

steps.title: For each TG, add 500 μl...
steps.instruction: For each TG, add 500 μl Buffer AW2 on top of the DNeasy Mini spin column. Do not touch the spin column with your pipette tip.

steps.title: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples
steps.instruction: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples. Make sure all your tubes are clearly labelled with your research group name and treatment group name. Make sure the centrifuge is balanced before you start. Check with a demonstrator about centrifuge usage if you are unclear.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 2 min at max speed
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 2 min at max speed.

steps.title: Discard the flow-through by pouring it...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through by pouring it into the waste collection container.

steps.title: Tap the collection tube on a...
steps.instruction: Tap the collection tube on a paper towel to remove the remaining liquid.

steps.title: Place the DNeasy Mini spin column...
steps.instruction: Place the DNeasy Mini spin column in the same 2ml collection tube.

steps.title: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples
steps.instruction: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples. Make sure all your tubes are clearly labelled with your research group name and treatment group name. Make sure the centrifuge is balanced before you start. Check with a demonstrator about centrifuge usage if you are unclear.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed.

steps.title: Remove the DNeasy Mini spin column...
steps.instruction: Remove the DNeasy Mini spin column from the collection tube carefully so that the column does not come into contact with the flow-through.

steps.title: Transfer the spin column to a new clearly labelled 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the spin column to a new clearly labelled 1.5 ml tube.

steps.title: Add 75 μl Buffer AE into...
steps.instruction: Add 75 μl Buffer AE into the centre of the DNeasy Mini spin column for elution. Make sure to pipette the buffer directly onto the membrane in the centre of the tube. The steps 5.46 to 5.56 elute the bound DNA from the silica membrane and bring it back into solution.

steps.title: Incubate for 5 min at room temperature (15–25°C)
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 min at room temperature (15–25°C).

steps.title: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples
steps.instruction: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples. Make sure all your tubes are clearly labelled with your research group name and treatment group name. Make sure the centrifuge is balanced before you start. Check with a demonstrator about centrifuge usage if you are unclear.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed.

steps.title: Add another 75 μl Buffer AE...
steps.instruction: Add another 75 μl Buffer AE into the centre of the DNeasy Mini spin column for elution. Make sure to pipette the buffer directly onto the membrane in the centre of the tube.

steps.title: Incubate for 5 min at room temperature (15–25°C)
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 min at room temperature (15–25°C).

steps.title: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples
steps.instruction: Team up with other research groups to centrifuge your samples. Make sure all your tubes are clearly labelled with your research group name and treatment group name. Make sure the centrifuge is balanced before you start. Check with a demonstrator about centrifuge usage if you are unclear.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 1 min at max speed.

steps.title: Discard the DNeasy Mini spin column
steps.instruction: Discard the DNeasy Mini spin column.

steps.title: Close and keep the collection tube
steps.instruction: Close and keep the collection tube.

steps.title: The DNA is now contained in your in your elute
steps.instruction: The DNA is now contained in your in your elute.

steps.title: You should have now five clearly...
steps.instruction: You should have now five clearly labelled collection tubes with about 150ul DNA in elution buffer. Each tube contains the DNA of one TG.

steps.title: Hand over your five clearly labelled...
steps.instruction: Hand over your five clearly labelled tubes to the demonstrator for storage till next week.
